sections.paragraph: Egyptian pr.n., given to Joseph in his public capacity by Pharaoh, Gen 41:45. The genuine Egyptian form of the word appears to be more accurately given by the LXX. Ψονθομφανήχ, in which Egyptian scholars (see Bernard, on Joseph. Ant. ii. 6, § 1 Jablonskii Opuscc. i. p. 207-216) recognize the Egyptian ⲡⲥⲱⲧⲙ ⲫⲉⲛⲉⲍ salvation, or saviour of the age, from ⲡ the article, ⲥⲱⲧ, σώζειν, σωτήρ, σωτηρία, and ⲫⲉⲛⲉⲍ αἰών. So Schol. Cod. Oxon. Ψονθομφανήχ, ὅ ἐστιν σωτὴρ κόσμου, and Jerome, servator mundi. The Hebrews interpreted the Hebrew form of the word, revealer of a secret, see Targ., Syr., Kimchi. [See also Thes.]
